caption: The video discusses the importance of Open Educational Resources (REA) in education, highlighting their unique characteristics and benefits. The frames show a slide with text in Spanish that asks why open educational resources are important in education and what makes them special. The slide also includes a definition of REA, explaining that they are teaching, learning, and research materials in the public domain or published under an intellectual property license that allows for free use, adaptation, and distribution. The 4R framework—Revisar (Revise), Redistribuir (Redistribute), Combinar (Remix), and Reutilizar (Reuse)—is prominently displayed, emphasizing the flexibility and adaptability of these resources. Additionally, the slide mentions that REA can include content, software, and open licenses, underscoring their comprehensive nature in supporting educational needs. The video is likely part of an educational presentation by CEDec, the National Center for Curriculum Development in Non-Proprietary Systems.
